pdfs.title: CARDIOLOGY CONSULTATION LETTER
pdfs.facility: Delaware Valley Cardiology Associates — 1200 Walnut St, Philadelphia PA

pages.heading: Re: Walter Reyes  DOB 11/03/1957
lines: Date of consultation: 12/30/2025
Referring provider: Dr. A. Patel (Primary Care)
Dear Dr. Patel,
Thank you for referring Mr. Reyes for evaluation of left bundle branch block
identified on routine ECG 11/2024 and mildly reduced ejection fraction.
IMPRESSION:
1. Chronic LBBB with LVEF 45-50% (TTE 12/09/2025) - etiology not yet defined.
Ischemic evaluation is INCOMPLETE. Stress imaging was ordered but the
patient deferred scheduling. I have re-emphasized its importance.
2. Paroxysmal atrial fibrillation, CHA2DS2-VASc 4 - continue apixaban 5 mg BID.
3. QTc mildly prolonged at 452 ms - avoid additive QT-prolonging agents.
RECOMMENDATIONS: Complete pharmacologic stress imaging within 4-6 weeks.
Low threshold for ischemic workup should he develop exertional symptoms.

pages.heading: ATTACHMENT: Transthoracic Echocardiogram Report 12/09/2025
lines: LV: LVEF 45-50%. Mild global hypokinesis. No regional wall motion
abnormality definitively identified (limited by LBBB dyssynchrony).
LA: Mildly dilated. RV: Normal size and function.
Valves: Trace MR. No AS/AI. No pericardial effusion.
Interpreting cardiologist: R. Whitman, MD, FACC

pages.heading: Patient: Reyes, Walter  68M
lines: CHIEF COMPLAINT: Chest pain, onset ~07:10 while mowing lawn.

6/10 on EMS arrival after rest.

Transmitted to receiving facility.
MEDICATIONS: None administered by EMS. ASA deferred to ED at pt request
(nausea).

pdfs.title: EXERCISE STRESS ECHOCARDIOGRAM REPORT (OUTSIDE RECORD, 09/2024)

pages.heading: Patient: Walter Reyes  DOB 11/03/1957  Study date 09/17/2024
lines: INDICATION: Dyspnea on exertion; pre-dated LBBB diagnosis.
PROTOCOL: Bruce. Exercised 6:24, 7.1 METS, 84% MPHR. Stopped for fatigue.
FINDINGS:
Rest: Normal LV wall motion, EF 55%.
Peak stress: Subtle hypokinesis of the inferolateral wall, EQUIVOCAL for
ischemia. No chest pain during test. Blunted BP response.
IMPRESSION: Equivocal study. Mild inferolateral hypokinesis at peak stress.
Recommend correlation with symptoms; consider coronary CTA or invasive
angiography if clinical suspicion persists.
*** NO RECORD OF FOLLOW-UP TESTING AT THIS FACILITY. ***
